title: Level 993

Level 993: “The Sky's Limit”
{{User:JakitoFan/Template:Class 0|class=Samim|hexagon=☁|header=Level Classified As - |safety=Distinctly Safe|security=Perfectly Secure|entity=Negligible Hostile Presence|color-h=200|color-s=100%|color-l=92.35%}}
== Description == === The Living Spaces === {{Blurquote|image=Cloud9.jpg|quote-text="Welcome," said he, "to our house, and when you have partaken of food you shall tell us what you have come for."|person=Telemachus, ''Homer's 'The Odyssey'''}} {{Blurimage|file=Sky..jpg|caption=An image of Level 993.|hovertext=For once in your life, it's not a window!}}{{Blurimage|file=Level993LivingRoom.png|caption=A less-furnished image of Level 993.|hovertext=Another photo of paradise.}} Level 993, colloquially known as 'The Sky's Limit', initially manifests as a one-story house, roughly twelve thousand kilometers long and wide, created of empty rooms separated by thin walls. This area is known as the 'Living Spaces'. These walls are made of drywall and air, and puncturing through it reveals the skeleton of the house, being a cacophony of wooden beams going every which way. The interior of these walls are of course inconsistent with the actual width. Within the randomly segmented rooms the furniture wildly varies. Some rooms may be bare, others may have various amounts of furniture. The main similarity between these rooms is that they consistently contain at least one window. While certain windows can be [[Entity 2|hostile]], these windows appear lethargic and have very slow reaction times. They can also be easily distinguished from normal windows by the fact that [[Entity 2|hostile windows]] will invariably show an inky black void. Due to the slowness of these [[Entity 2|windows]] and them being easily avoided, they pose virtually no threat to wanderers. Certain rooms may have couches, these usually have different forms of currency tucked away within the cushions. These can vary from [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] money like Euros and Yen, to Backrooms currencies such as the [[Republic of Aurielle|Auriellen Nolic]]. These currencies seem to regenerate after some time, making them goldmines for potential monetary gain. === The Sky's Limits === {{Blurquote|image=CLOUDS.gif|quote-text=Yet He commanded the clouds above, and opened the doors of heaven...|person=Asaph, ''Psalms 78:23''}} One can exit this initial area by climbing out of any window. Doing this will reveal the second area, known as the 'Sky's Limits'. This area typically manifests as a platform constructed of infinite clouds. These clouds have gaps that one can fall through, so take care when traversing. Notably, an ever-persistent phenomenon prevents no-clipping unless it is consciously done. The clouds are described as exceptionally soft, like a wool blanket. Notably, unlike typical clouds, one can actually stand on these. Plentifully along the clouds one can find unique buildings, which will be grouped and listed below. |-|Offices= {{Blurimage|file=Office_view.jpg|caption=A second-floor view of a Meeting Room in Level 993.|hovertext=Meeting rooms.}}These structures manifest as large office buildings. They usually fluctuate between ten to twenty stories high. These buildings usually consist of large glass hallways with doors on either side, leading to different rooms. The most common of these are the meeting rooms, which usually have a large glass window overlooking the scenery. They usually have a large meeting table, counters with drawers underneath, and several office chairs. On the counter one can usually find an array of office supplies and coffee pots, which contain [[Object 40|Work Juice]]. Another common room is the Cloud Offices. These manifest as rooms with wooden walls and sky-based wallpaper. They usually have a large desk and chair, filled with office supplies, trinkets, and foodstuffs. Each desk is equipped with a large 2010's-era computer with two to three monitors. These computers are equipped with full internet access, allowing one to access the entirety of the [[Phenomenon 2|Back-Net]]. Rarely, these computers can also access search engines, but not any websites; meaning one can search for modern events but cannot access further information beyond brief summaries. By far the rarest of these rooms are the Break Rooms. These usually have counters, tables and desks, and large floor-to-ceiling windows. Coffee pots and bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] are strewn on the counters. Typically, one can find wallets filled with money from different nations, [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]] or [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. Usually, the total ranges around $500 to $7500 USD. These rooms are useful due to their monetary and utilitarian stockpiles. |-|Stores= {{Blurimage|file=Sky's the Limit.jpg|caption=A store within Level 993.|hovertext=Stocked Foods.}}These structures manifest as large, two-story stores. These stores are usually associated with a brand called 'SkySpace'. Within these stores are aisles of different items. These items usually range from foodstuffs such as bags of chips or bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], to useful objects such as multipurpose tools and kitchen utensils, to even monetary items such as coins and bills. All of these items are priced at $0.00. For unknown reasons, if a person unfamiliar with USD views these tags, the tags will display a price of 0 in their native currency. For example, a person from the [[Republic of Aurielle]] would see a price tag of 0 ₦. While there are checkout counters, no one staffs them, except by choice. All items in the store are branded under an organization called 'Skyspace'. Usually, one may find wanderers amongst the aisles, searching for specific items or simply enjoying themselves. Stores rarely have two stories, with a second story being a large living space, akin to the Living Spaces area. These houses are usually stocked and occupied by either wanderers, [[Entity 9|Facelings]], or an infestation of [[Kats|Kawfold]]. These Stores are important and treasured by occupants, which usually resist efforts to demolish. Usually, Stores have the same layout, for which follows: {| class="wikitable" |+ Aisles of a Store |- ! Aisle # !! Contents |- | 1-3 || Fruits, vegetables, organic foods |- | 4-6 || Snacks, chips, junk foods |- | 7 || Desserts, ready-made meals, plastic utensils |- | 8-9 || Miscellaneous [[Category:Safe|safe]] [[Object List|objects]] (eg. [[Object 1|Almond Water]], [[Object 27|Mixologist's Delight]]) |- | 10-11 || Kitchenware |- | 12-17 || Home renovation tools, furniture, countertops |- | 18 || Weaponry, hunting tools |- | 19-20 || Trinkets, children's toys |- | 21 || Board games, card games |- | 22 || Video game consoles, video games |- | 23-25 || Memorabilia from different levels (eg. Wallpaper from [[Level 0]]) |- | 26 || Official store/brand merchandise |} |-|Bars= {{Blurimage|file=Cloud Refresh.jpg|hovertext=Mmmm, (Cloud) Refreshing!|caption=A glass of Cloud Refresh, commonly found amongst the drinks at a Bar.}}These establishments, nicknamed 'Bars' or 'Pubs', are more rare compared to others. These establishments serve exactly two drinks, called Cloud Milk and Cloud Refresh. Cloud Milk stands excessively gelid, radiating a soft glow and holding a texture fitting flawlessly to the sky. It imparts an unfamiliar sensation to those who consume it, and they declare it discerns like harboring in the sky. It is unassailable to drink and seems to be unsusceptible to [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]. As one gazes into the container, one might notice delicate, wispy patterns gently swirling within the liquid. These patterns evoke shifting shapes and tranquility of accurate clouds, counting a sense of movement to the milk's appearance. Cloud Refresh is a soft drink similar to those on Earth. When ingested, it seems to stimulate the imagination and creativity of the wanderer utilizing the product, inspiring their artistic side. Furthermore, it can also quench the wanderer's thirst, living up to its name. This product's form differs from wanderers; it's almost abstract, existing within clouds. Yet, reports on the diverse appearances of this product remain untitled and unverified. Indeed, this product seems fascinating, although it could conceal hidden truths. Conversely, the taste of this product is exceedingly sweet, even sweeter than Almond Water, yet this sugar seems to have no adverse effects on the wanderer's health. Overuse of Cloud Refresh can lead to debilitating and possibly fatal addictions. The glasses these drinks are served in are usually covered with a SkySpace logo. The bar is usually constructed of blue-painted wooden panels with cloud murals. The barstools are comfy, with a plush blue top and ornate wood for the stool portion. Tables and chairs are spread throughout the bar, constructed in the same manner as barstools. Typically, a grouping of wanderers may be found throughout the bar. |-|Homes= {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2025-12-26 212235.png|hovertext=A place to live out your dreams.|caption=A photograph of the exterior of two homes, and two floating clouds.}}Homes are the primary residences of the many wanderers throughout the level. They manifest as large, one-to-three story houses floating anywhere between 30mm to 5ft off of the clouds. These houses are lushly furnished, being perfect places to relax. Usually, there are at least 6 different unique room types present within the houses, though these rooms vary. Typically, the largest houses are used by multiple people as to not be greedy. {| class="wikitable" |+ Room Types of a Home |- ! Room Name !! Purpose/Furnishings |- | Living Rooms || These rooms are usually equipped with a large couch, a TV, a few chairs, a small glass table, four to five paintings, and potted plants. These rooms are intended for the wanderer's leisure, to relax and lounge around during the day. |- | Dining Rooms and Kitchens || These rooms are usually equipped with a large ornate blue wooden table, six to eight wooden chairs, cabinets with cookingware and silverware, plates, an oven, sink, fridge, pantry, and microwave. These rooms are intended for the wanderer to get some sustenance, or have a dinner with friends. A noteworthy comment is that fridges and pantries automatically replenish. |- | Miscellaneous Rooms || These rooms may have paintings, windows (either regular windows or [[Entity 2|innocuous windows]]), a chair or two, and varying other furnitures. These rooms do not fit into any one category. |- | Bedrooms || These rooms are usually equipped with an Alaskan King bed, windows with blackout curtains, plugs with nightlights, several pillows and blankets, heated comforters, lamps, two bedside tables with cabinets, and entrances to Closets. These rooms are intended for the wanderer to rest in, and relax for the night. Notably, if the wanderer is on any medication, it will automatically appear in a cabinet in the bedside tables. |- | Closets || These rooms are usually equipped with several wardrobes' worth of clothing items and chests for storage. These rooms are usually intended for the wanderer to pick outfits or change in. Notably, these clothes differ depending on the room. They are consistently found adjacent to Bedrooms. |- | Backyards || These rooms stretch the definition of 'room'. These rooms are consistently found at the edges of a house and consist of a picket fence painted in a random muted shade, fake grass, lawn chairs, and very rarely a small hot tub. These 'rooms' are intended for the wanderer to spend their leisure time in. |- | Breaker Rooms || Typically, there is only one of these rooms per house. They contain a breaker which controls the house's power, with specifically labeled switches. These rooms also contain a high-powered router, enough to provide maximum bars of internet in a roughly ⅝th mile range. Notably, labels near the breaker's switches will appear in the wanderer's native language, or the one they are most fluent in. If the language does not have a written form, a button will be present which will describe what the switch does. |- | Bathrooms || Equipped with a bathtub, a sink, a shower, a series of cabinets, towel racks equipped with soft fluffy towels, and a toilet paired with toilet paper. These rooms are intended for the wanderer to relieve themselves in, or take a relaxing shower to clean themselves properly. |- | Leisure Rooms || These rooms usually contain large bean bag seats, couches, futons with board games underneath, at least one high-powered gaming PC, miscellaneous gaming consoles, and several small tables or cabinets. These rooms are intended for wanderers to relax in, play games, contact other people, or have a board game night. |- | Balcony Rooms || Akin to the Backyards, this room stretches the definition of 'room'. The room usually takes the form of a spacious balcony, usually roughly 12ft from wall to railing. These balconies usually have lounging chairs, tables, air conditioning, and drinks that automatically replenish themselves. This 'room' is used for relaxing and looking at the scenery. == Contemporary History == {{Blurimage|file=Woodrooms to 993.png|hovertext=Enter a cloudy paradise.|caption=An exit from [[Level 27]] to Level 993, photographed c. 2012.}}This level was discovered around the year 1893, presumably near New Year's Eve. An anonymous wanderer reported on the [[Phenomenon 2|Back-Net]] that he had discovered a door here from [[Level 27]], engraved with a cloud symbol. [[Kats|Kawfold]] were presumably introduced to the level around 1912, imported by wanderers; they became a staple of the level. The level's existence was confirmed on 12/11/1968, at this time the human population was roughly ~200 persons. In celebration of New Year's Eve 1999, the [[M.E.G.]] and the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] announced a joint base in the level, referred to as Shared Base ₳. Over the years, more groups joined. Currently, all major groups within the BackroomsStrictly excluding the [[User:ArbiTraitor/AEG Rewrite|AEA and AEG]], as to avoid the extinction of the level's entities are collaborating in this base, with several groups withdrawing and re-entering over the years in accordance with conflicts between groups. Shared with very few levels, Level 993 was only affected by [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] for est. 1 week, after which collaborative efforts by its population returned the level to normalcy. During this time, no foreign entities were recorded, and those present in the Level beforehand hitched rides on Drifting Clouds to avoid the dark ground. == Entities == Aside from previously-mentioned entities, there is one level-exclusive entity. {{Entity Class|no=993|Hab=Level 993, [[Level 993.1]]|Agg=0|Int=2|Dan=1}}{{Blurimage|file=Cloud9.jpg|hovertext=Say hello! :D|caption=A Drifting Cloud, photographed in [[Level 993.1]].}} Entity 993, typically known as Drifting Clouds, are entities that occasionally appear within the level. They are solid clouds that lazily drift across the level. They can be used as a method of transport throughout the level. These Drifting Clouds may occasionally rise and fall, which may create a falling hazard. You should take care to hold on when this happens. Drifting Clouds are not aggressive in any way. Usually, they are kept as pets, and are capable of ingesting food and drinkIt should be noted that while they do not need to eat, they enjoy small treats.. They are overall friendly, lighthearted creatures that deserve care and affection. Notably, they also emit a small amount of light. == Colonies and Outposts == === Shared Base ₳ === ▷Experiment between fourteen different [[Groups|groups]], collaborative effort between them ▷Roughly ~260 members ▷Closed to independent traders, open to traders associated with a [[Groups|group]] ▷''Technically'' open to new members, must be strictly approved by at least ⅔rds of the residents. === Cloudy Days Organization === ▷Founded to help new wanderers adjust, also experts in medical aid ▷~300 permanent members, ~500 additional volunteers at any given time ▷Open to trades, wealthy in supplies ▷Open to volunteers, permanent members must undergo a screening process === SkySpace ===
File:SKYSPACE.bck
{{Blurimage|file=SkyspaceChips.png|caption=An image of Skyspace-branded chips lying in a stole.|hovertext=Skyspace!}} SkySpace, also formatted as Skyspace, SKYSPACE or skyspace, is a prevalent brand within Level 993. Skyspace branding is spread throughout the level, appearing on all objects in Stores and most objects found elsewhere. SkySpace is also notable for hosting by far the largest colony in the Level, hosting anywhere between ~800 to ~1000 members, referred to as 'employees', at any given time. While SkySpace has no real corporate leadership, there are several subdivisions that employees are assigned to by employers. {{Collapse|initial=▷Open departments|final=▽Close departments|body= ▶Store Clerks are found in Stores, usually staffing the checkout counters or greeting wanderers. ▶Bartenders are found in Bars, mixing up fresh drinks for patrons who enter and greeting others. ▶Home Care are found in Homes, and typically help disabled, ill or aged wanderers to live out their life. ▶Officeworkers are found in Offices, typically relaxing in break rooms or using printers for advertisements.}} Employers are the individuals who conduct screenings for new members, and also the ones to direct them to whatever department they are intended to be at. While there is no salary, employees have special rights to any currencies that may spawn within Offices they are assigned to, and major discounts in stores. They are by far one of the most important groups in the level.
== Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== *Noclipping through the clouds of the [[Level_399#The Plane of Clouds|Cloud Plane]] in Level 399 may lead one here. *Going incredibly high into the sky in any Backrooms level is theorized to eventually bring one here. *Going into blue wooden doors carved with a '☁' in certain levels may bring one here. *It is theorized there is a direct entrance from [[Level 385|Levels 385]], [[Level 386|386]], and [[Level 387|387]]; however this is unconfirmed. ===Exits=== *Falling through the clouds can bring one either to [[The Void]], [[Level 385]], [[Level 386]], [[Level 387]], [[Level 0]], or [[Level 399]]. *Riding a drifting cloud through the level may cause one to enter [[Level 993.1]]. *No-clipping through the clouds typically leads one to a sky-related level. *No-clipping in buildings have varying results. **No-clipping in offices direct one most often to [[Level 4]], **No-clipping in bars direct one most often to [[Level 610]], **No-clipping in houses direct one most often to [[Level 201]] or [[Level 231]], **And No-clipping in stores directs one most often to [[Level 711]]. __NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:☁The Sky's Limit☁}} {{Background|file=Blue Sky, Dhaka, Bangladesh.jpg}} [[Category:Sky]][[Category:Safe]][[Category:Safe Haven]][[Category:Groups]][[Category:Objects]][[Category:Entities]][[Category:Levels]][[Category:Cluster I]] {{CSS|content= .wiki-tools .wds-button.wds-is-secondary { --wds-secondary-button-label-color: #8e8ead; Can't believe it, but you found me. Now, I guess I owe you some author information. The original page was made by [[User:TheAgentAnonymous|Apyr]], and revamped by [[User:JakitoFan|me]]. All images were either taken from the original page or edited by me in Canva. Thanks for reading! Don't forget, this page is the reason why we know what the plural for Kats are. It's Kawfold.}}
